patches.find: Dome's own H(r) implies 90% gravity drop at the rim; observed variation is 0.53% (170× error)
patches.replace: Dome's own H(r) implies 90% gravity drop at Antarctic rim; apples-to-apples measurement yields 1,430× error (170× using whole-globe spread as conservative lower bound)
patches.rationale: Lead with the apples-to-apples 1,430× figure from detail_extra, which compares predicted Antarctic-rim drop to observed Antarctic drop. 170× uses the global 0.53% pole-equator spread as denominator — a conservative understatement. Makes argument internally consistent.

patches.find: The dome's own height formula predicts gravity drops to ~10% of normal at the Antarctic rim. Real measurements show gravity varies by only 0.5% across the whole globe (9.780 m/s² at the equator to 9.832 m/s² at the pole) — a discrepancy of 170× with the dome's predicted variation. To explain this, the dome invokes an undefined 'Sub-terrestrial structure' correlation (Domain 19 of live-power page) with no published mechanism or equations.
patches.replace: The dome's own height formula predicts gravity drops to ~10% of normal at the Antarctic rim. At 80°S, measured gravity is 9.826 m/s² — just 0.006 m/s² (0.063%) below the polar reference. Dividing the dome's predicted 8.87 m/s² drop by this 0.006 m/s² measured drop gives 1,430×. Using the conservative whole-globe pole-equator spread as denominator instead still yields 170×. The dome invokes an undefined 'Sub-terrestrial structure' correlation (Domain 19 of live-power page) with no published mechanism or equations.
patches.rationale: Replace vague '170× discrepancy' with the apples-to-apples 1,430× calculation, citing specific Antarctic gravity values. Retain 170× as the conservative lower bound.

patches.find: The dome's own formula predicts 90% gravity loss at the rim; actual variation is 0.5% — off by a factor of 170. The 'Sub-terrestrial structure' escape hatch has no equations and no measurements.
patches.replace: The dome's own formula predicts 90% gravity loss at the Antarctic rim. Comparing to the actual Antarctic measurement gives a 1,430× error (using the global 0.5% spread as denominator gives a conservative 170×). The 'Sub-terrestrial structure' escape hatch has no equations and no measurements.
patches.rationale: Lead with 1,430× (apples-to-apples) and explain 170× as the conservative variant. Consistent with finding and tldr_evidence updates.

patches.find: (L2) Mohe timing — WIN-011 (1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse)
patches.replace: (L2) Mohe timing — WIN-011 (Wang et al. 2000, 1997 Mohe eclipse)
patches.rationale: Wang et al. paper published 2000 (Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101); 1997 is the eclipse event date. Fix to avoid conflation. Hole 5 from SEC-8b.3.c4.

patches.find: with 1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse data
patches.replace: with Wang et al. 2000 (1997 Mohe eclipse) gravity data
patches.rationale: Second occurrence of same citation error. Wang et al. published 2000; 1997 refers to the eclipse.
